description:
**Problem**
The WAL implementation (~8,300 lines) lives in \`beads-rs/src/daemon/wal/\` while the WAL contracts and memory index already live in \`beads-daemon-core/src/wal/\`. This split means:
1. The WAL subsystem spans two locations — you can't reason about it by reading one place
2. The WAL impl is trapped inside the beads-rs monolith, blocking the daemon runtime extraction
3. Tests couple to \`beads_rs::daemon::wal::*\` internals instead of a stable crate boundary

**Design**
Move the WAL implementation from \`beads-rs/src/daemon/wal/\` into \`beads-daemon-core/src/wal/\`, alongside the existing contracts. Feature-gate heavy dependencies so model/fuzz consumers can avoid pulling in rusqlite.

Move map:
- \`beads-rs/src/daemon/wal/event_wal.rs\` → \`beads-daemon-core/src/wal/event_wal.rs\`
- \`beads-rs/src/daemon/wal/frame.rs\` → merge with existing \`beads-daemon-core/src/wal/frame.rs\` (or rename)
- \`beads-rs/src/daemon/wal/fsck.rs\` → \`beads-daemon-core/src/wal/fsck.rs\`
- \`beads-rs/src/daemon/wal/index.rs\` → \`beads-daemon-core/src/wal/sqlite_index.rs\`
- \`beads-rs/src/daemon/wal/memory_wal.rs\` → \`beads-daemon-core/src/wal/memory_wal.rs\`
- \`beads-rs/src/daemon/wal/record.rs\` → \`beads-daemon-core/src/wal/record.rs\`
- \`beads-rs/src/daemon/wal/replay.rs\` → \`beads-daemon-core/src/wal/replay.rs\`
- \`beads-rs/src/daemon/wal/seams.rs\` → \`beads-daemon-core/src/wal/seams.rs\`
- \`beads-rs/src/daemon/wal/segment.rs\` → \`beads-daemon-core/src/wal/segment.rs\`
- \`beads-rs/src/daemon/wal/mod.rs\` → update \`beads-daemon-core/src/wal/mod.rs\` to include new modules

Feature gating in \`beads-daemon-core/Cargo.toml\`:
\`\`\`toml
[features]
default = []
wal-sqlite = ["dep:rusqlite"]
wal-fs = []  # enables segment writer, replay, fsck (filesystem IO)
test-harness = ["uuid"]

[dependencies]
rusqlite = { version = "0.31", optional = true }
rand = { version = "0.9", optional = true }
# crc32c already present
\`\`\`

Temporary compat shim in beads-rs:
\`\`\`rust
// beads-rs/src/daemon/wal/mod.rs (compat re-exports)
pub use beads_daemon_core::wal::*;
\`\`\`

**Acceptance**
- [ ] All WAL implementation files moved to \`beads-daemon-core/src/wal/\`
- [ ] \`beads-daemon-core\` compiles with default features (contracts + memory index only)
- [ ] \`beads-daemon-core\` compiles with \`wal-sqlite\` + \`wal-fs\` features (full WAL impl)
- [ ] \`beads-rs\` depends on \`beads-daemon-core\` with \`wal-sqlite\` + \`wal-fs\` features enabled
- [ ] Compat shim in \`beads-rs/src/daemon/wal/mod.rs\` preserves existing import paths
- [ ] WAL unit tests moved to \`beads-daemon-core\` or still pass from beads-rs via re-exports
- [ ] \`cargo check --workspace\` passes
- [ ] \`cargo test --workspace\` passes
- [ ] \`just dylint\` passes

**Files:** \`crates/beads-daemon-core/src/wal/\` (expand), \`crates/beads-daemon-core/Cargo.toml\`, \`crates/beads-rs/src/daemon/wal/\` (becomes compat shim), \`crates/beads-rs/Cargo.toml\`
title: Phase III: absorb WAL implementation into beads-daemon-core
Label/note data leaks across bead ID collisions - LabelStore/NoteStore merge before collision resolution, losing lineage data visible on winner

description:
**Problem**
WAL files are stored under the daemon socket dir (often XDG_RUNTIME_DIR). Those locations are ephemeral and can be cleared on reboot/cleanup. If sync hasn't completed, a reboot can drop WAL and lose acknowledged mutations.

**Design**
Store WALs in a persistent data directory (XDG_DATA_HOME/beads-rs/wal). Keep socket in runtime dir but decouple WAL path. Add migration from legacy runtime WAL dir to new persistent dir on daemon startup, preserving newer WAL if both exist. Allow env override for tests.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] WAL persists across daemon restarts/reboots (no runtime dir dependency).
- [ ] Legacy runtime WALs are migrated or preserved with a warning.
- [ ] Tests cover migration + legacy format.
- [ ] Tests pass.

**Files:** src/daemon/wal.rs, src/daemon/run.rs, src/daemon/ipc.rs
title: Persist WAL in durable data dir
